title: Satellite-measurednighttimelightintensityspikesindicateincreasedtechdatacenteractivity
thesis: Sudden increases in nighttime light emissions from known data center locations suggest heightened cloud computing demand. XLK includes major cloud and data center operators whose revenues correlate with data usage intensity.
data_sources: NASA Black Marble Nighttime Lights API
signal_logic: If 3-day average nighttime light intensity in data center regions rises >10% vs prior month average
entry_exit: Entry: If 3-day average nighttime light intensity in data center regions rises >10% vs prior month average Exit: After 7 trading days or if intensity falls below prior month average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use NASA Black Marble Nighttime Lights API via api (daily).

schema.idea: Satellite-measured nighttime light intensity spikes indicate increased tech data center activity

data.source: NASA Black Marble Nighttime Lights API

schema.behavior: Sudden increases in nighttime light emissions from known data center locations suggest heightened cloud computing demand.

market_impact.rationale: XLK includes major cloud and data center operators whose revenues correlate with data usage intensity.

trade_logic.entry: If 3-day average nighttime light intensity in data center regions rises >10% vs prior month average
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days or if intensity falls below prior month average
